leagueBriefing: UConn extended its historic winning streak to 48 games in a Big East blowout, while South Carolina and Texas set up a heavyweight SEC championship rematch.

games.headline: UConn's dominance continues with 48th straight win in Big East rout
games.brief: Top-ranked UConn left no doubt in their Big East quarterfinal opener, dismantling Georgetown to extend their incredible winning streak to 48 games. Serah Williams sparked an early 10-0 run that effectively ended the contest before halftime, as the Huskies' suffocating defense held the Hoyas to just one field goal in the entire third quarter. The win marks UConn's 37th consecutive conference tournament victory and moves them one step closer to another trophy.
bullets: Serah Williams led the charge with 14 points, 12 of which came in the first half.
Sarah Strong tallied 11 points and reached 100 steals for the season, a rare UConn feat.
The Huskies' defense held Georgetown to just 18 points in the first half.
UConn has now won 43 consecutive games against the Hoyas.
The victory extends UConn’s overall winning streak to 48 games.
games.context: UConn advances to the Big East semifinals to face Creighton as they hunt for another conference title.

games.headline: Duke survives Notre Dame's late-game surge to reach ACC title game
games.brief: In a heart-pounding ACC semifinal, top-seeded Duke managed to withstand a furious comeback attempt from Notre Dame to secure a spot in the championship. Hannah Hidalgo nearly willed the Irish to victory with 24 points and a clutch late steal, but Duke's Taina Mair hit the triple that ultimately kept the Blue Devils ahead. Notre Dame had three looks at the basket in the final 11 seconds but couldn't find the equalizer in a classic postseason battle.
bullets: Hannah Hidalgo led all scorers with 24 points and 8 rebounds.
Taina Mair paced Duke with 16 points, including a critical late 3-pointer.
Duke held on despite Notre Dame taking the lead midway through the third quarter.
The Irish outscored Duke in the fourth but fell two points short.
Duke advances to face the Louisville-North Carolina winner for the ACC title.
games.context: Duke heads to the ACC Championship while Notre Dame awaits its NCAA Tournament seeding.

games.headline: UCLA sets program record with 24th straight win to reach Big Ten final
games.brief: UCLA continued its historic season by leaning on its stars to down Ohio State and reach the Big Ten championship game. Kiki Rice led five Bruins in double figures as the team secured its 24th consecutive victory, a new school record. Despite a 23-point effort from the Buckeyes' Chance Gray, UCLA led wire-to-wire, using a dominant first-half defensive performance to build a cushion that Ohio State could never quite overcome.
bullets: Kiki Rice led the Bruins with 17 points; Lauren Betts added 14 points and 9 rebounds.
UCLA set a school record with its 24th consecutive victory.
The Bruins held Ohio State to just 28% shooting in the first half.
Chance Gray kept the Buckeyes in it with a game-high 23 points.
UCLA advances to face No. 9 Iowa in a blockbuster Big Ten final.
games.context: UCLA moves into the Big Ten Championship game against Iowa with an eye on the No. 1 overall seed.

games.headline: Princeton explodes in fourth quarter to clinch Ivy League crown
games.brief: Princeton left no doubt about who rules the Ivy League, turning a close game into a blowout in the fourth quarter to secure the regular-season title. Fadima Tall and Madison St. Rose combined for 39 points, leading a 26-point final-frame onslaught that buried Yale. The Tigers clinched the top seed for 'Ivy Madness' as Columbia stumbled elsewhere, solidifying Princeton's status as the team to beat in the conference.
bullets: Fadima Tall led the Tigers with 22 points on 8-of-10 shooting.
Madison St. Rose contributed 17 points as Princeton pulled away late.
Princeton outscored Yale 26-13 in the fourth quarter to seal the title.
The Tigers shot a clinical 16-of-18 from the free-throw line.
Princeton clinches the No. 1 seed for the upcoming Ivy Madness tournament.
games.context: Princeton secures the Ivy regular-season title and the top seed for the conference tournament.

games.headline: Berry’s double-double powers Louisville into ACC Championship
games.brief: Louisville backup guard Imari Berry stepped into the spotlight when it mattered most, delivering a 22-point double-double to lead the Cardinals past North Carolina. In a game of runs, Berry provided the spark every time the Tar Heels threatened, including six critical points in the fourth quarter that pushed the lead back to double digits. The win snaps a hot streak for UNC and sets up a championship date with top-seeded Duke.
bullets: Backup guard Imari Berry led Louisville with 22 points and 10 rebounds.
Berry hit 4-of-8 from 3-point range to fuel the Cardinal offense.
North Carolina’s 13-game winning streak in recent weeks was halted.
Louisville held UNC to just 27 points in the first half.
The Cardinals sealed the game by hitting key free throws in the final minute.
games.context: Louisville advances to face No. 1 Duke for the ACC Tournament title.

games.headline: Suarez and Big 12 champ TCU end Kansas State's Cinderella run
games.brief: The Horned Frogs showed why they were the regular-season Big 12 champions, pulling away in the second half to end 12th-seeded Kansas State’s historic tournament run. Marta Suarez took over in the third quarter, scoring 14 of her 22 points to break open a tie game. Despite the Wildcats' defensive pressure and early lead, TCU's dominance on the glass (27-12) and interior scoring ultimately proved too much for a K-State team playing its fourth game in as many days.
bullets: Marta Suarez scored a game-high 22 points, including 14 in the third quarter.
Olivia Miles added 18 points as TCU moved to 29-4 on the season.
TCU dominated the rebounding battle 27-12.
Kansas State was the first No. 12 seed in Big 12 history to reach the semifinals.
The Horned Frogs held K-State to just 25% shooting in the final quarter.
games.context: TCU advances to the Big 12 title game to face West Virginia in a rematch of tournament finalists.

games.headline: Gamecocks fend off LSU comeback to reach fourth straight SEC final
games.brief: In a heavyweight battle that felt like a national final, South Carolina leaned on its depth and poise to outlast LSU in Greenville. Raven Johnson hit a career-high 22 points, but it was Joyce Edwards who took over late, scoring 15 second-half points to blunt an LSU rally led by MiLaysia Fulwiley. The win keeps the Gamecocks' hopes for a fourth straight SEC tournament title alive and sets up a high-stakes championship clash with Texas.
bullets: Raven Johnson scored a career-high 22 points to lead South Carolina.
Freshman Joyce Edwards dominated the second half with 15 of her 18 points.
MiLaysia Fulwiley led LSU with 24 points against her former team.
South Carolina used a 10-2 run in the third quarter to regain control.
LSU failed to foul in the final minute, allowing the Gamecocks to run out the clock.
games.context: South Carolina advances to the SEC Championship for a rematch against Texas.

games.headline: Iowa’s fourth-quarter explosion buries Michigan to reach Big Ten final
games.brief: Iowa used a devastating 24-6 fourth-quarter run to dismantle Michigan and secure a spot in the Big Ten championship game. After the Wolverines took a lead into the final frame, Hannah Stuelke sparked a 15-2 Hawkeye burst that completely changed the game. Michigan set program records for wins this season, but they were held to a season-low point total as the Hawkeyes' defense clamped down when it mattered most.
bullets: Ava Heiden led Iowa with 16 points; Hannah Stuelke added 13 points and 10 rebounds.
Iowa outscored Michigan 24-6 in the fourth quarter.
Michigan was held to a season-low 42 points in the loss.
Hannah Stuelke scored seven points in the first two minutes of the final quarter.
Michigan's Mila Holloway was held to 10 points on 4-of-13 shooting.
games.context: Iowa advances to the Big Ten final for a high-profile matchup with No. 2 UCLA.

games.headline: West Virginia survives defensive battle to reach Big 12 title game
games.brief: In a physical, low-scoring affair that came down to the final second, West Virginia edged Colorado to punch their ticket to the Big 12 final. Gia Cooke proved to be the hero, grabbing a late offensive rebound and hitting the go-ahead triple with 38 seconds left. Colorado had a chance to win at the line, but a missed free throw and a late steal by Jordan Harrison sealed the escape for the Mountaineers.
bullets: Gia Cooke hit the go-ahead 3-pointer with 38 seconds remaining.
Jordan Harrison led West Virginia with 15 points and the game-sealing steal.
Both teams were held scoreless for the final six minutes of the second quarter.
West Virginia blew a 12-point lead before rallying late in the fourth.
Colorado's Zyanna Walker led all scorers with 16 points.
games.context: West Virginia advances to play TCU in the Big 12 Championship game.

games.headline: Madison Booker’s 31 points carry Texas back to SEC Championship
games.brief: Texas used a dominant fourth-quarter performance from superstar Madison Booker to pull away from a resilient Ole Miss squad. Booker was everywhere, finishing with 31 points and 11 rebounds as the Longhorns outscored the Rebels 28-13 in the final frame. Ole Miss had cut the lead to two points heading into the fourth, but they had no answer for Booker, who scored 13 in the final ten minutes to set up a rematch with South Carolina.
bullets: Madison Booker dominated with 31 points, 11 rebounds, and 5 assists.
Texas outscored Ole Miss 28-13 in the fourth quarter.
Kyla Oldacre added 19 points as the Longhorns won the paint battle 30-14.
Mississippi’s Cotie McMahon and Denim DeShields each scored 20 points.
Texas advances to their second straight SEC Tournament title game.
games.context: Texas sets up a championship rematch against No. 3 South Carolina.
